methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Durin is a Pre-Seed stage infrastructure and logistics company that has raised $3.4M to date. The relevant peer universe for this sector includes two private comparables (AiDash and Watoga Technologies), though neither operates at the same stage or valuation tier, limiting direct peer median benchmarking.

Given sparse same-stage comps in Infrastructure & Logistics, Stage-Progression methodology is the primary applicable triangulation approach. Using a $12.0B total raised proxy as the base and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Pre-Seed advancement yields an implied valuation of $30.0B. This single-method result constrains the range to $25.5B–$34.5B, with $30.0B as the point estimate. The Peer-Median approach was not applied due to insufficient narrow peer datapoints within the same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier.

The core uncertainty in this analysis stems from methodological concentration: reliance on a single triangulation method and the use of total raised as a valuation proxy introduces risk. The Stage-Progression multiplier and the $12.0B base assumption merit sensitivity testing. A more robust valuation would require either additional pre-seed stage comps in infrastructure logistics or alternative methodologies (discounted cash flow, venture capital method). The current range of $25.5B–$34.5B should be treated as preliminary pending stronger comparables or operational milestones.
